findings.title: Disguised TODO: 'Future: replaced by LLM-generated responses'
findings.description: This comment describes planned future work ('Future: replaced by LLM-generated responses') rather than explaining why the current code exists. It functions as an implicit TODO without using the forbidden keyword. It will mislead future maintainers who will not know whether LLM integration has already happened elsewhere, and it commits to a specific implementation path (LLM) that may change. Comments should explain the current design rationale, not future intentions.
findings.suggestion: Remove the forward-looking statement. If the template-based approach is intentional for the walking skeleton, document why templates were chosen (e.g., 'Template-based generation avoids LLM dependency during initial integration.') without promising a specific future replacement.

findings.title: Comment references specific future tech (LanceDB) that will rot
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: defines the contract for future LanceDB implementation.
findings.description: This comment couples the interface documentation to a specific storage technology (LanceDB) that may or may not be adopted. Once any vector store is implemented -- whether LanceDB, Qdrant, Pinecone, or something else -- this comment becomes misleading. Interface documentation should describe the contract's purpose, not a planned implementation.
findings.suggestion: Replace with: 'Contract for vector storage operations. Implementations may use LanceDB, pgvector, or any embedding-capable store.' Or simply remove the technology reference: 'Contract for vector storage operations used by the context engine.'

findings.title: Walking skeleton scope comment will rot as triggers are added
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: only T03 (rest-reminder) is mapped.
findings.description: This comment is tightly coupled to the current implementation scope ('only T03 is mapped'). As soon as additional triggers are mapped (T04, T05, etc.), this comment becomes stale and misleading. Scope annotations like this have a very short useful lifespan and high maintenance cost.
findings.suggestion: Remove the scope annotation. The code is self-documenting -- the Record contents clearly show which triggers are mapped. If context is needed, use a more durable comment: 'Maps trigger names to the emotion states they induce.'

findings.title: Walking skeleton scope comment will rot on response-generator
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: only T03 (rest-reminder) templates are defined.
findings.description: Comment states 'only T03 templates are defined' which will become false as soon as another trigger's templates are added. The RESPONSE_TEMPLATES object is directly readable and self-documenting about which triggers it covers.
findings.suggestion: Remove the scope annotation. Replace with a durable description: 'Template-based response messages keyed by trigger name and emotion state.'

findings.title: Walking skeleton scope comment on setupContextEngine will rot
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: only initializes screenshot capture.
findings.description: Comment states 'only initializes screenshot capture' which will become stale when additional context sources (activity tracking, vector store, etc.) are wired in. The function body is short enough to be self-explanatory.
findings.suggestion: Remove the scope annotation or replace with a durable description: 'Initialize the ContextEngine service for the main process, wiring platform-specific providers.'

findings.title: Walking skeleton scope comment on setupPersonaEngine will rot
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: only T03 rest-reminder trigger is active.
findings.description: Comment states 'only T03 rest-reminder trigger is active' which will become false as soon as additional triggers are registered. The triggers array in the function body clearly shows which triggers are active.
findings.suggestion: Remove the scope annotation or replace with: 'Initialize the PersonaEngine service for the main process, registering proactive triggers and managing emotion state.'

positive_observations: No forbidden comment patterns (TODO, FIXME, HACK, XXX, PLACEHOLDER, TEMP) found in any of the 11 files
JSDoc parameter descriptions accurately match actual function signatures across all files
Interface comments on types.ts files explain 'why' (e.g., 'Intentionally decoupled from context-engine' on TriggerInput) rather than just restating the type structure
Duration function has excellent docstring explaining the continuity gap semantics -- valuable 'why' documentation
Clear architectural annotation on ScreenshotCapture: 'This is the Imperative Shell' helps maintainers understand the design pattern
